title: shared_inbox_uri stored without SSRF validation
description: In server.rs handle_inbox Follow branch, inbox_uri gets an SSRF guard (lines 574-582) but shared_inbox_uri from actor_doc["endpoints"]["sharedInbox"] is stored raw without any validation (line 567-569, 595). During delivery fan_out, shared_inbox_uri takes priority as the delivery target (delivery.rs line 76). An attacker-controlled actor document can set endpoints.sharedInbox to a private/internal IP (e.g. 169.254.169.254) that will be stored and then later POSTed to by the delivery worker, bypassing the guard that was added for inbox_uri. Fix: apply the same https-only + is_private_host check to shared_inbox_uri before storing it, or reject the whole Follow if shared_inbox_uri is present but invalid.
close_reason: Fixed: shared_inbox SSRF guard, key_owner required, webhook media cap. Remaining (replay, unsigned accept, DNS rebinding, body buffer) accepted as known architectural limitations.
